title: With Our Polluted Mind How To Bring About Purification Of Consciousness
speaker: HG Amitasana Dasa

segments.text: Some fruitive, my mind is acting in that direction only, some oner, something I want out of it.
So with polluted consciousness, how to practice this thing, how to bring this element of detachment?
So this purification of consciousness is inbuilt in the system.
One thing is the world has been designed so beautifully by the Lord,
when you act in wrong way, it will backfire.
When you want to act as an enjoyer, act as a proprietor, it will hit you back.
Even as devotees.
That's why Krishna said,
After many times getting hit, finally I said,
And now I realize I am not the enjoyer.
So we will start doing devotional service with that same consciousness, I am the enjoyer.
I want to enjoy peace of mind.
I want to enjoy lording over bhaktas.
We start with that.
Cannot help it.
Theoretically it will not help.
And today onwards I will not enjoy.
And today onwards I will not think I am the proprietor.
It doesn't happen.
It will be at a mental level.
So we engage in devotional service, just like a diamond gets rubbed.
So this rubbing will go on here also.
So we will collect, we will think that I am a big collector.
Finally you will get a hit.
No, I am not the collector.
You go for book distribution, one day you will distribute a lot of books.
You get a feeling, I am a good, I am a very expert, very nice.
See I convinced him.
Next day you do all your convincing and nobody will buy a book.
Then you realize, my God, it's a fact, I am not the convincing.
This happens over a period of time.
And while we are engaged in devotional service, slowly, dhire dhire, dhire dhire, that purification happens.
One comes to our senses.
Unfortunately, to some extent you can fast track the process, otherwise it will take time.
That's why it's a dhairiya, patience.
You start with potency.
You start with polluted consciousness.
As we engage, the purification of consciousness is inbuilt in the system.
For that, what we have to do, we have to, we have to, the process, shravanam, hearing,
shunvata, swakataha, krishna, punya, shravana, kirtana,
nashta, praya, shobhadreshu, nityam, bhagavata, sevaya.
When we do that, all this abhadras, dirty things which is clinging to us, clinging to our consciousness,
which is overpowered our consciousness, completely gets subsided.
Just like diamond, it's actually a very precious stone, but when you see original, this thing, it is unpolished, uncut,
it is not shiny, but because of that polish, because of rubbing, because of different treatment it goes through,
it starts shining.
Same is the case what happens with us.
